widgets_values:
Resolution must be divisibile by 16

The starting image loaded here is only used in the first video clip.

However, you should keep it set in order for the image resize node to work properly.
(or you can connect the end image to the "resize image resolution to" node)

Increasing Motion Latent Count and Overlap may help in situations where you can't get smooth SVI clip transitions.

overlap should be 1 + 4 * motion latent count.
(e.g. for each motion latent count increased, increase overlap by 4)

This only applies to continued clips.
Make sure to assign your SVI LoRA file paths here!!!
Set this number corresponding to the batch# folder you want to acceess, to continue the video clip from there.
- Index serves to segment the batch files that are created, incrementing each time the workflow is run, so that each output is saved in a new folder.

>>> IMPORTANT <<<
You need to set your Comfyui output directory.

E.g. mine was:
(since I have comfyui installed through pinokio)
C:\pinokio\drive\drives\peers\d1764384723056\output\

(Optional) You can also set where you want the files to be saved in your output folder.
IMPORTANT: This must end with \batch#

This workflow uses my own custom nodes for saving and loading latents, which I've provided along with the workflow on my civitai page.
https://civitai.com/models/2534583/wan-22-i2v-comfyui-workflow-svi-fflf-with-latent-saving-and-loading
https://civitai.com/user/bvga

I haven't uploaded the nodes to github or anywhere else. So that's the only official place you'll find them.

Simply drag the bvga_nodes folder into your custom nodes folder, and they should work.

nodes.title: Negative Prompt
widgets_values: 色调艳丽，过曝，静态，细节模糊不清，字幕，风格，作品，画作，画面，静止，整体发灰，最差质量，低质量，JPEG压缩残留，丑陋的，残缺的，多余的手指，画得不好的手部，画得不好的脸部，畸形的，毁容的，形态畸形的肢体，手指融合，静止不动的画面，杂乱的背景，三条腿，背景人很多，倒着走

Credit to LastAssignment, whose workflows finally got SVI working for me, and I learned a lot from.
https://civitai.com/models/2232205/wan-22-i2v-comfyui-workflow-svi-extend-flf2v-upscale

Thes 3-k-sampler setup was also his idea, if you want to read more about it:
https://civitai.red/articles/26433/wan-22-and-the-quest-for-the-best-motionquality-balance-with-the-least-amount-of-steps

== How this workflow functions:

- This is a SVI workflow that enables first-frame-last-frame for continued smooth videos.

- What is special about this workflow is that it saves the latent as a file, and loads these into the workflow so you can freely manage what video clip you want to tinker with, as much as you'd like.

== Workflow Setup:

- Assign your model and file paths (Blue nodes).
^ THIS IS IMPORTANT; you need to manually set the Comfyui Output Directory path.

- Configure your desired settings (Red nodes)

- Adjust the particular settings you want for the video clip segment you're generating (Green nodes)

== Running the Workflow:

There are two workflows here. One for a Starting Clip, and one for Continued Clips. Only enable one at a time.

For the video video segment you generate, enable the "Starting Clip Workflow".
This will generate an initial clip for you to build from.

For continued video clips, enable the "Continued Clip Workflow".
Set the "File Batch Selected" node value to the batch# file you want to load.

About this workflow:

The aim of this 3-k-sampler workflow is to minimize the color-shift that occurs when using SVI and FFLF, which is in-part due to imprecise scheduling and lightning loras reducing quality.

It uses a 3-k-sampler approach to help remedy this.
Much of the color shift is introduced in the first few steps of the high noise, so we use a "base sampler" without the lightning loras to prevent much of this. While the remaining high and low scheduler still feature the lightning loras to get reasonable generation speeds.

>>>IMPORTANT<<<
Because of this, you will need to use a checkpoint model that doesn't have the lightning loras baked in.

Additionally, the sampler settings have been optimized for the best quality.
You can reduce base step multiplier to 1 to get slightly faster generation speeds, at a bit of a quality loss, without affecting this balance.
But if you tweak any of the sampler settings, such as increasing or lowering steps, be aware that you'll need to find different ratios to get the best quality.
